assertions.title: Search response time under 500ms for FTS

steps.action: curl with timing: GET /api/search?q=kalshi (default, no type param)
curl with timing: GET /api/search?q=tailscale (default, no type param)
curl with timing: GET /api/search?q=JWT (default, no type param)
Verify default response has empty semantic array
steps.expected: semantic array is empty when no type param specified
steps.observed: semantic count: 0 (empty). Messages: 20, facts: 20, sessions: 10 returned.

steps.expected: Slower response with semantic results included
steps.observed: Response time: 1706ms (HTTP 200), semantic count: 5. Confirms type separation works.

evidence.network: GET /api/search?q=kalshi -> 200 (7.0ms), GET /api/search?q=tailscale -> 200 (14.5ms), GET /api/search?q=JWT -> 200 (3.2ms), GET /api/search?q=kalshi&type=fts -> 200 (6.2ms), GET /api/search?q=tailscale&type=fts -> 200 (4.2ms), GET /api/search?q=JWT&type=fts -> 200 (2.8ms), GET /api/search?q=kalshi&type=all -> 200 (1706ms)

summary: Tested 1 assertion (VAL-SEARCH-009): PASS. All 6 FTS queries completed in 2.8ms-14.5ms, well under the 500ms threshold. The type parameter fix (commit 50215e9) successfully separates FTS from semantic search — default queries return FTS-only results (semantic array empty) while type=all includes semantic results at ~1.7s.
